block.html:
Knowledge.

Overview.

DNA is a double helix. 2 strands

Each strand is a chain of nucleotides

At each position, 2 bases interact forming a base pair - ie. A and G

A nucleotide has one base (A/C/G/T) and a sugar/phosphate chemistry to keep it together.

groups of 3 bases are referred to as a codon

RNA polymerase reads DNA one base at a time. There is no triplet framing.

The ribosome reads mRNA in non-overlapping triplets. That is where codons function as codons.

mRNA is then translated into a protein

Each codon (3x bases) maps to one amino acid or a stop codon (special token which signals the end of protein synthesis)

The codons are chained together using peptide bonds

This forms a polypeptide chain

When the stop codon is encountered, the translation process stops

The polypeptide chain folds into a functional 3D structure (protein folding)

This is referred to as its “native state”

Degeneracy

Codons are 3 nucleotides, each which have a base (A,C,G,T)

There are 64 possible triplet combinations (codons) using the four bases (A, U, G, C)

Each codon encodes one amino acid

An amino acid can be encoded by multiple codons

For instance, codons GAA and GAG both specify glutamic acid and exhibit redundancy

This is referred to as degeneracy

Mathematically:

4^3 = 64 codons

22 amino acids

respiration → ATP → powers RNA polymerase which moves along DNA → assembles RNA → ribosome grabs RNA → it steps along RNA and uses ATP to bring in amino acid and produce → peptide bond of amino acids → chain of amino acids together → folds into a protein → protein

DNA is a chemical polymer made of nucleotides.

A nucleotide is one “letter” (A,C,G,T) plus sugar+phosphate (the chemistry).

DNA is usually double-stranded: two complementary letters bind (A–T, C–G).
One matched pair across the strands is a base pair.

A gene is a stretch of DNA letters.

When expressed, DNA is read in groups of 3 nucleotides.
A 3-letter group is a codon.

Each codon maps to one amino acid (or stop).

Amino acids are small chemicals (20 main types) that link into a chain.

That amino-acid chain is a protein.

So:
nucleotides → DNA sequence (base pairs in double helix) → codons (triplets) → amino acids → protein.
